pattern: Since every index can be reached from any earlier index whose value is close enough, the longest jump count to reach each position only depends on the best count already found at the positions before it.
hints: Work left to right and track, for each index, the largest number of jumps needed to reach it so far -- start with zero jumps to reach index zero and treat every other index as unreachable at first.
For each pair of indices i < j where the two values differ by no more than target, index j can be reached one jump after index i, so its best count can be updated from index i's best count.
The answer is whatever count ends up stored for the final index, or -1 if it was never updated from its initial unreachable state.

edge_inputs.description: smallest array with an unreachable huge gap
strictly increasing values force one jump per step
all equal values allow jumping to every index in turn

stress.description: random values with a moderate target so both reachable and unreachable cases occur
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 1000))
    nums = [rng.randint(-1000000000, 1000000000) for _ in range(n)]
    target = rng.randint(0, 2000000000)
    return [nums, target]
